project: Cloud Worker Orchestration Pipeline with Final Artifacts, Optional PR Creation, and Worker CLI Wiring
description: Implement a deterministic cloud worker orchestration pipeline that claims runs, executes setup and workflow logic, persists final workflow-specific artifacts, optionally creates PRs for auto/review, and finalizes run/attempt states without regressing existing CLI help, JSON contracts, or redaction guarantees.

userStories.title: Fix atomic claim attempt-count semantics in persistence adapters
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want ClaimRun to atomically increment attempt_count and enforce eligibility filters so attempt numbering is deterministic under worker contention.
acceptanceCriteria: Postgres ClaimRun atomically selects an eligible run, sets status to claimed, and increments attempt_count in a single statement.
Turso ClaimRun provides equivalent atomic select/claim/increment behavior.
Claim selection excludes runs where cancel_requested=true or attempt_count has reached max_attempts.
Store contract tests verify attempt_count increments exactly once on a successful claim and does not increment when no run is claimed.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add worker orchestration composition root
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a dedicated worker orchestration package with injected dependencies so the run loop is testable and isolated from Cobra wiring.
acceptanceCriteria: A worker orchestration package exists with explicit configuration and dependency injection boundaries.
The package exposes a run-loop API that polls for claims and dispatches single-attempt execution.
Context cancellation stops the loop gracefully without leaving partial in-flight state transitions.
Unit tests cover idle polling, one successful dispatch, and graceful shutdown behavior.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Orchestrate deterministic setup pipeline for claimed attempts
userStories.description: As a worker, I want setup to run in a fixed sequence so every claimed attempt starts from a valid sandbox and auth state.
acceptanceCriteria: Setup executes in strict order: provision sandbox, bootstrap repo/init, materialize auth, then run preflight.
Transition from claimed to running uses valid transition checks with conflict-safe update behavior.
Setup failures emit failure events and set deterministic run/attempt outcomes.
Retry evaluation is invoked for setup failures using existing retry classification logic.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Execute workflows with heartbeat renewals and cancel propagation
userStories.description: As a worker, I want long-running workflow execution to renew leases and honor cancel intent so active attempts are safely controlled.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow dispatch maps run, auto, and review to the correct execution command paths.
Heartbeat renewal and cancel checks run at a configurable interval during active execution.
When cancel_requested is observed, the active attempt transitions to canceled and the run transitions to canceled.
Lease renewal failure, lease_lost, and profile_revoked conditions terminate attempts with the expected error-code semantics.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Collect final artifacts by workflow kind and persist final snapshots
userStories.description: As a cloud user, I want final snapshots to contain the correct artifact groups for each workflow so cloud pull restores expected files.
acceptanceCriteria: Final artifact collection uses workflow-specific groups: run=state and auto/review=state+reports.
Artifact collection enforces allowlist and path-safety protections, including symlink protections.
Final bundle manifests use ComputeBundleHash and are persisted with SnapshotKindFinal.
Run snapshot references are updated to final snapshot/version while preserving original input snapshot reference.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add optional idempotent GitHub PR creation for auto/review
userStories.description: As an operator, I want optional PR creation for auto/review workflows so merge-ready changes can be surfaced automatically without affecting run workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: PR creation executes only for auto/review workflows when enabled in worker configuration.
Run workflows never trigger PR creation side effects.
PR creation uses existing idempotency semantics so retries do not create duplicate PRs.
PR side-effect events are emitted through existing redaction-aware event paths.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Deterministic finalization and cleanup across terminal outcomes
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want terminal transitions and cleanup to be deterministic so runs cannot end in ambiguous states.
acceptanceCriteria: Success path finalizes with attempt=succeeded and run=succeeded after final snapshot handling.
Failure paths finalize retryable and non-retryable outcomes consistent with existing failure classification.
lease_lost and profile_revoked paths finalize with expected error codes and lock-release behavior.
Sandbox teardown and auth-lock release are attempted on all terminal paths with best-effort semantics.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add orchestration scenario tests for happy path and required failures
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want scenario-driven tests so worker state-machine regressions are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: Table-driven orchestration scenarios cover happy path, cancel, lease_lost, profile_revoked, and setup failure.
Each scenario asserts final run status, final attempt status, and required event emissions.
Scenarios verify final snapshot presence or absence according to outcome.
Tests verify ComputeBundleHash remains deterministic and does not mutate input data.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire a worker CLI entrypoint with testable command handlers
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a CLI command to run the worker loop so cloud workers can be started and configured explicitly.
acceptanceCriteria: A worker command surface is added with flags for worker identity and loop intervals.
The Cobra handler delegates to a testable run-command helper with injectable factories and writers.
The command supports bounded/single-run mode for deterministic test execution.
Unit tests cover flag validation, startup errors, and successful execution.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preserve existing command/help output and cloud JSON/redaction contracts
userStories.description: As an automation consumer, I want existing CLI help and JSON outputs to remain stable while worker support is added.
acceptanceCriteria: Help text for existing cloud commands (setup, run, auto, review, status, logs, pull, cancel, auth) remains valid with no regressions.
Existing cloud JSON response key contracts for already-supported commands remain unchanged.
Worker and lifecycle outputs continue to pass through redaction chokepoints with no secret leakage in human or JSON output.
Regression tests assert help-surface compatibility and redaction/JSON contract stability.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
